mnemonics.title: SINGLY: Single Independent Node Goes Link
mnemonics.content: SINGLY = Single Independent Node, Go Link (to) Next. It reminds you that a singly linked list consists of nodes that only have a next pointer; you start at HEAD and move forward along the next links.

mnemonics.title: DOUBlY: Double Pointers Prev & Next
mnemonics.content: DOUBlY = Double (two) pointers: Prev and Next. In a doubly linked list each node has both a previous and a next pointer, allowing traversal in both directions.

mnemonics.title: CIRCULAR: Circle In Real Chain
mnemonics.content: CIRCULAR = Circle In Real Chain. The tail's next points back to the head, forming a closed loop instead of null.

mnemonics.title: HEAD & TAIL: H & T
mnemonics.content: HEAD points to the first node; TAIL (if maintained) points to the last node. Useful for O(1) insertion at the end.

acronyms.usage: General term for any linear collection of nodes linked by pointers.
Use when you need forward-only traversal and memory efficiency.
Use when you need to traverse backward and forward, or need O(1) deletion given a node.
Used for round-robin scheduling, buffers, or problems requiring cyclic iteration.
Points to the first node; the starting point for any traversal.
Points to the last node; optional but helpful for O(1) insertion at the end.
Represents the end of a non-circular list; in singly/doubly lists the last node's next/prev points to NULL.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a line of people holding hands, each person (node) extending their right hand to the next person; the first person is the HEAD, the last person points to nothing (NULL).
Picture a two-way hallway of rooms; each room has a door to the previous room and a door to the next room, allowing movement both forward and backward.
Think of a ring of beads on a string: the string loops from the last bead back to the first bead; there is no loose end.
Imagine a circular train track where each carriage has doors on both sides linking to the previous and next carriage, forming a closed loop.

analogies.analogy: A train of carriages connected only by a coupling to the next carriage; you can only travel forward from the engine (HEAD) to the last carriage.
A double-ended chain of people holding hands with both left and right hands; they can move forward or backward along the line.
A carousel (merry-go-round) where each seat (node) is connected to the next seat in a circle; after the last seat you return to the first.
A node is like a person, a pointer is like a hand pointing to the next person; NULL is an empty hand indicating no one else to point to.
